command:
cd /private/tmp/vsdd-S-3.03 && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(S-3.03): implement block-ai-attribution WASM hook

Ports block-ai-attribution from bash to native WASM (S-3.03).

- detect_attribution(command): pure-core pattern scan; returns Some(label)
  for 11 AI attribution patterns, None for clean input (BC-4.05.004)
- on_hook_logic: gates Bash/git-commit commands only; returns Block with
  human-readable reason when pattern matched, Continue otherwise
  (BC-2.01.002, BC-4.05.001, BC-4.05.002, BC-4.05.003)
- 9 canonical patterns: Co-Authored-By:{Claude,Anthropic,GPT,OpenAI,
  Gemini,Google AI}; Generated with Claude Code; Generated by Claude;
  Generated by AI; noreply@anthropic.com; noreply@openai.com
- Case-insensitive; handles extra whitespace after Co-Authored-By: colon
- Near-miss non-matches: human Co-Authored-By, bare "generated", human
  name "claude" outside attribution context
- hooks-registry.toml: replace legacy-bash-adapter entry with native
  hook-plugins/block-ai-attribution.wasm; on_error=block preserved
- clippy clean (allow dead_code on WASM entry symbol); fmt applied

43/43 tests passing.
EOF
)"

.factory/specs/prd.md (lowercase) as canonical, per the case-sensitivity
incident in the v1.0-brownfield-backfill cycle (pass-9 APFS data loss).

Historical adversarial-review and log files are not retroactively edited.
EOF
)" && git push origin develop

docs: clarify spec file naming convention (lowercase prd.md)

command:
gh pr create --base develop --head docs/spec-file-naming-convention --title "docs: clarify spec file naming convention (lowercase prd.md)" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- Appends a **Spec File Naming** section to `CONTRIBUTING.md` establishing `.factory/specs/prd.md` (lowercase) as the canonical PRD path.
- Documents the rationale (case-insensitive APFS incident in the v1.0-brownfield-backfill cycle pass-9, which caused uncommitted Wave 11 prd.md edits to be lost when `git rm -f specs/PRD.md` deleted the shared inode).
- Establishes that historical adversarial-review and log files are not retroactively edited.

## Why a separate PR
This is a docs-only follow-up to the Semgrep SAST adoption (PR #22, merged). It was inadvertently left uncommitted in the working tree during that change. Splitting it out keeps the diff focused.

## Test plan
- [x] `git diff` shows only an 11-line addition to `CONTRIBUTING.md`
- [x] No code or workflow changes
- [x] Section formatting matches the surrounding markdown style (`---` separator + `## Spec File Naming` heading)

## Impact
- Documentation only.
- Zero behavioral or build-system change.
- Zero impact on Wave 11 in-flight work (S-4.09 + S-4.10 RED gates already committed in their own worktrees).
EOF
)"
